findings.description: privateKeyWIF parameter in buildTransaction() — JVM String is immutable, so key cannot be zeroed after use. This is an inherent JVM limitation, acceptable for MVP. Callers should minimize WIF lifetime in scope.

estimateFees() catches all exceptions and falls back to defaults silently. This is intentional — matches web wallet behavior and prevents fee estimation failures from blocking transactions. Fallback rates are safe (conservative defaults).
DUST_SAT=546 correctly matches Bitcoin's standard dust threshold. Fee minimum prevents miners from ignoring transactions.
Transaction signing uses SIGHASH_ALL|ANYONECANPAY=true — standard for single-signer P2PKH transactions. No security concern.

summary: No security vulnerabilities found. Private key handling is standard for JVM. Fee fallback behavior is safe. All constants match Bitcoin protocol standards.
